questions.question: Explain the concept of 'global state' in the context of distributed systems. Provide an example to illustrate your explanation.
questions.hint: Global state refers to the complete set of information about the system at any given time.

questions.question: Describe the difference between 'synchronous' and 'asynchronous' global states in distributed systems. Provide an example to support your explanation.
questions.hint: In synchronous global state, all processes are synchronized before proceeding, while in asynchronous global state, processes proceed independently.

questions.question: A distributed system consists of three processes: A, B, and C. Process A has a global state of (X, Y) and sends a message to process B with its current value of X. Process B then updates its global state to (X, Z) and sends a message to process C. Process C's initial global state is (P, Q) and receives the message from process B with value X. What is process C's new global state after receiving the message from process B?
